summary: provider.ts is a 1916-line monolith combining SDK resolution, custom loaders, model catalog, and LRU caching
MCP OAuth tokens potentially stored without encryption
80+ BUILTIN_TOOL_IDS creates tool sprawl with overlapping functionality (smart_bash vs shell, smart_grep vs grep)
CRITICAL: smart_bash uses spawnSync(shell:true) with unsanitized user input — shell injection risk from naive substring filtering bypass
Two parallel tool systems coexist: primary (permission-gated, truncation, typed results) vs smart_ tools (spawnSync, no permissions, no truncation, no file locks)
Placeholder tools (github_pr_search, github_triage) in production registry return non-functional responses
CRITICAL: network.request capability defined but NOT enforced at runtime — no fetch proxy exists, any plugin can exfiltrate data via raw fetch()
CRITICAL: filesystem.read and filesystem.write capabilities entirely unenforced — defined in types but never checked anywhere, no HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP entries
experimental.text.complete hook is network-gated but missing from HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP — shouldDispatch returns false for unknown hooks → always denied
TOCTOU race in plugin trigger() — registrations snapshot captured but capability check uses live Ref state
